metadata.summary: Initial semi-automatic baseline. Scores are curated from public benchmark and adoption signals, with strict separation between technical capability and AI-led autonomy.

modules.name: Model intelligence
modules.summary: Frontier models are strong at language, code, and reasoning, but factuality and self-verification remain limits for world-level delegation.

criteria.name: General benchmark strength
criteria.rawValue: Composite view of public model leaderboards and AI Index technical performance
criteria.normalization: Curated 0-100 score; high benchmark capability receives credit but is discounted for benchmark saturation.
criteria.notes: Useful for broad technical capability, not direct autonomy.

criteria.name: Code and reasoning performance
criteria.rawValue: SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, and reasoning benchmark signals
criteria.normalization: Curated score emphasizing contamination-resistant and real-task benchmarks.
criteria.notes: Strong but still brittle outside benchmark framing.

criteria.name: Factual reliability and self-checking
criteria.rawValue: Known hallucination, citation, and self-correction limitations
criteria.normalization: Curated penalty for residual hallucination and weak self-verification.
criteria.notes: Reliability is the largest reason capability does not translate into leadership.

criteria.name: Long-context consistency
criteria.rawValue: Long document and multi-file workflow behavior
criteria.normalization: Curated score based on practical use rather than nominal context length.
criteria.notes: Large windows help, but consistent global reasoning is still uneven.

modules.name: Multimodal content
modules.bottleneck: Generation is strong, production control is still human-led
modules.summary: Image, audio, video, and document models are useful in production, but humans still drive intent, quality control, and distribution.

criteria.name: Image and document understanding
criteria.rawValue: OCR, chart, image, and document model capability
criteria.normalization: Curated score from public multimodal model performance.
criteria.notes: Useful for analysis workflows, less reliable for high-stakes extraction.

criteria.name: Video and audio generation
criteria.rawValue: Frontier media generation systems
criteria.normalization: Curated score with penalty for controllability and consistency limits.
criteria.notes: Creative output is impressive, but workflow control is not autonomous.

criteria.name: End-to-end content production control
criteria.rawValue: Planning, generation, editing, publishing, and feedback loops
criteria.normalization: Curated autonomy-discounted score.
criteria.notes: Adoption is broad, but humans still own editorial judgment.

modules.name: Scientific discovery
modules.bottleneck: AI assists science more than it autonomously discovers
modules.summary: AI meaningfully accelerates research workflows, especially in biology and materials, but autonomous discovery loops remain early.

criteria.name: Research assistance capability
criteria.rawValue: Literature, hypothesis, coding, and analysis assistance
criteria.normalization: Curated score based on observed scientific workflow usefulness.
criteria.notes: Strong as a collaborator, weaker as an independent scientist.

criteria.name: Closed-loop lab automation
criteria.rawValue: Autonomous experiment design, execution, and validation
criteria.normalization: Curated score with strict autonomy requirement.
criteria.notes: Promising systems exist, but broad reliable autonomy is not present.

criteria.name: Validation and reproducibility
criteria.rawValue: Ability to produce validated, reproducible discoveries
criteria.normalization: Curated score for reproducibility and independent verification.
criteria.notes: Validation remains a human-heavy bottleneck.

modules.name: Compute infrastructure
modules.bottleneck: Energy and supply chains constrain scaling
modules.summary: Training and inference infrastructure continues to scale quickly, but power, chips, memory, and data center capacity are binding constraints.

criteria.name: Frontier training compute
criteria.rawValue: Training compute trend and frontier model scale
criteria.normalization: Curated score from Epoch trends.
criteria.notes: The supply side is advancing quickly.

criteria.name: Inference affordability
criteria.rawValue: Model serving cost and availability signals
criteria.normalization: Curated score based on cost declines and deployment friction.
criteria.notes: Costs are improving but remain meaningful for sustained autonomy.

criteria.name: Energy and data center capacity
criteria.rawValue: AI data center power demand and grid constraints
criteria.normalization: Curated score where constraints reduce readiness.
criteria.notes: Power availability increasingly shapes deployment.

criteria.name: Chip and memory supply chain
criteria.rawValue: GPU, accelerator, HBM, and fabrication supply constraints
criteria.normalization: Curated infrastructure readiness score.
criteria.notes: Scaling is not limited by algorithms alone.

modules.name: Agent execution
modules.summary: Agents can complete constrained tasks, especially coding issues, but sustained autonomous execution remains fragile.

criteria.name: Single issue repair
criteria.rawValue: SWE-bench Verified style repository tasks
criteria.normalization: Curated score discounted for benchmark limitations.
criteria.notes: Best current evidence for practical agent task completion.

criteria.name: Long task time horizon
criteria.rawValue: METR task-completion time horizon framing
criteria.normalization: Strict autonomy score based on long-task bottlenecks.
criteria.notes: The core metric for whether agents can work without constant supervision.

criteria.name: Tool failure recovery
criteria.rawValue: Terminal, browser, API, and CI recovery behavior
criteria.normalization: Curated score from observed workflow reliability.
criteria.notes: Recovery depends heavily on project verification infrastructure.

criteria.name: Low-supervision execution
criteria.rawValue: Hours of useful work without human intervention
criteria.normalization: Curated strict autonomy score.
criteria.notes: Most real deployments still require frequent human review.

modules.name: Tools and workflows
modules.summary: Tool calling and IDE integration are mature enough for assistance, but end-to-end workflows still rely on human-owned verification.

criteria.name: Tool use maturity
criteria.rawValue: Function calling, browser use, terminal use, and API integration
criteria.normalization: Curated score for availability and stability.
criteria.notes: Tool use is mainstream, but tool outcomes still need checking.

criteria.name: Workflow integration
criteria.rawValue: IDE, SaaS, GitHub, Slack, CRM, and internal tool integration
criteria.normalization: Curated score for real workflow adoption.
criteria.notes: Adoption exists, but deployment quality varies widely.

criteria.name: Verification infrastructure
criteria.rawValue: Tests, CI, sandboxing, rollback, and observability
criteria.normalization: Curated score for agent-ready verification.
criteria.notes: The limiting factor is often the environment, not only the model.

criteria.name: Autonomous orchestration
criteria.rawValue: Multi-step planning, retries, delegation, and state tracking
criteria.normalization: Curated strict autonomy score.
criteria.notes: Frameworks are improving faster than production confidence.

modules.name: Data and memory
modules.bottleneck: World state and enterprise memory are fragmented
modules.summary: AI has broad public knowledge but weak durable memory, provenance, and controlled access to private operational state.

criteria.name: Public knowledge coverage
criteria.rawValue: Pretraining and retrieval over public information
criteria.normalization: Curated score for broad knowledge availability.
criteria.notes: Public knowledge is abundant but not always current or grounded.

criteria.name: Enterprise data readiness
criteria.rawValue: Data quality, access control, lineage, and AI readiness
criteria.normalization: Curated score based on common enterprise blockers.

source.label: Met Office AI data readiness checklist

criteria.notes: Data governance remains one of the largest adoption blockers.

criteria.name: Persistent project memory
criteria.rawValue: Cross-task memory, continuity, and forgetting resistance
criteria.normalization: Curated early-stage autonomy score.
criteria.notes: Persistent operational memory is still a product and research frontier.

criteria.name: Provenance and auditability
criteria.rawValue: Ability to trace data and decision origins
criteria.normalization: Curated score for traceable AI memory.
criteria.notes: Traceability is essential for delegating high-stakes work.

modules.name: Robotics and physical world
modules.summary: Robotics adoption is real, but most robots are specialized automation rather than general embodied intelligence.

criteria.name: Industrial robot deployment
criteria.rawValue: Robot installation and density trends
criteria.normalization: Curated deployment score from industrial robotics signals.
criteria.notes: Deployment exists, but most systems are narrow.

criteria.name: General embodied intelligence
criteria.rawValue: General-purpose manipulation, navigation, and learning
criteria.normalization: Strict autonomy score for broad physical competence.
criteria.notes: The gap between demos and reliable general robots remains large.

criteria.name: Physical safety certification
criteria.rawValue: Safety cases for AI-controlled physical systems
criteria.normalization: Curated readiness score.
criteria.notes: Safety certification slows broad autonomy in the physical world.

criteria.name: Robot cost and supply readiness
criteria.rawValue: Hardware cost, availability, and maintenance burden
criteria.normalization: Curated deployment-readiness score.
criteria.notes: Cost and maintenance still constrain physical scale.

modules.name: Enterprise economy
modules.bottleneck: AI adoption has not become AI control
modules.summary: Enterprise use is widespread, but most value still comes from augmentation rather than AI owning core production functions.

criteria.name: Organizational AI adoption
criteria.rawValue: Surveyed enterprise AI and generative AI adoption
criteria.normalization: Curated adoption score.
criteria.notes: Adoption is no longer fringe.

criteria.name: Core production control
criteria.rawValue: AI ownership of core workflows and output accountability
criteria.normalization: Strict autonomy score.
criteria.notes: AI is usually embedded as assistance, not final ownership.

criteria.name: Scaled ROI evidence
criteria.rawValue: Evidence of scaled financial impact
criteria.normalization: Curated score for durable enterprise value.
criteria.notes: Many organizations are still between pilot and scaled value.

criteria.name: Labor substitution in critical workflows
criteria.rawValue: AI replacing rather than augmenting human work
criteria.normalization: Strict control score.
criteria.notes: Substitution exists in pockets, but broad control has not shifted.

modules.name: Safety and governance
modules.summary: Safety evaluation, incident tracking, and policy work are maturing, but the world lacks robust audit and control mechanisms for broad AI autonomy.

criteria.name: Incident monitoring
criteria.rawValue: AI incident and hazard tracking
criteria.normalization: Curated score for monitoring maturity.
criteria.notes: Tracking is improving, but completeness and taxonomy remain challenges.

criteria.name: Safety benchmark maturity
criteria.rawValue: Safety evaluations, red teaming, and responsible AI benchmarks
criteria.normalization: Curated maturity score.
criteria.notes: Benchmarks are improving but lag frontier deployment.

criteria.name: Regulatory readiness
criteria.rawValue: Policy, standards, audits, and compliance structures
criteria.normalization: Curated governance-readiness score.
criteria.notes: Frameworks exist, but operational enforcement varies.

criteria.name: System auditability
criteria.rawValue: Ability to inspect, replay, and govern AI decisions
criteria.normalization: Strict governance score.
criteria.notes: Auditability is not yet standard across AI workflows.
